title: The Secrets Behind the Construction of Angkor Wat • rooseveltvanderbilt.org
highlights: Wat? Labor was essential in the construction of Angkor Wat, as it required a vast workforce to transport materials, carve stone, and assemble the temple complex. Historical estimates suggest that tens of thousands of laborers, including skilled artisans and unskilled workers, were mobilized for this monumental project, which took several decades to complete. The Khmer Empire organized labor through a system of corvée, where subjects were required to contribute labor for state projects, reflecting the centralized authority and re  sources of the empire. This extensive labor force enabled the construction of the intricate architecture and elaborate carvings that characterize Angkor Wat, showcasing  ...    were organized into specialized teams based on their skills and tasks. Historical records indicate that these teams included stone masons, carpenters, and laborers, each responsible for specific aspects of the construction process. title: Angkor Wat
highlights:
## History

### Construction Angkor Wat was commissioned by the Khmer king Suryavarman II(ruled 1113–c. 1150) in the early 12th century in Yaśodharapura(present-day Angkor), the capital of the Khmer Empire. The construction of the temple commenced in 1122 CE and was completed in 1150 CE.
